English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
William T. Miller papers
Miller, William T. (William Taylor), 1911-1998.
14-8-3021
10 cubic feet. 10 cubic feet.
1941-1992. Correspondence, publications, research materials Papers, primarily relating to his research in fluorine chemistry, especially his work on the Manhattan Project at Columbia University. Also, files relating to the Cornell Chemistry Dept. and building projects in Baker Laboratory. Collection material in English 3800156
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2014
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2014
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, April 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Chemistry, Cornell University.
William Miller received a B.S. and a Ph.D. from Duke University, and came to Cornell in 1936. During World War II, he worked at the S.A.M. Laboratories of the Manhattan Project at Columbia University on highly stable fluorocarbon polymers that would not react with uranium gas, thus making possible the separation of the fissionable isotope at Oak Ridge. After the war he returned to Cornell where he taught until his retirement in 1977. In 1974 he received the American Chemical Society award for Creative Work in Fluorine Chemistry, and in 1986 the French Moissan Centenary Medal in honor of Henri Moisson, who discovered the element fluorine in 1886. In addition to his teaching and research at Cornell, Miller was active in the planning of the new research wing of Baker Laboratory and in reconstructing the teaching laboratories in the building.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Papers, primarily relating to his research in fluorine chemistry, especially his work on the Manhattan Project at Columbia University. Also, files relating to the Cornell Chemistry Dept. and building projects in Baker Laboratory.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Early Alpha Files
Series II. Copies of Notebooks SAM
Series III. Early SAM
Series IV. SAM Files 1
Series V. Alpha Files - Cornell A-L
Series VI. Alpha Files - Cornell M-Z and Chemistry Department Newsletters
Series VII. Baker Lecture Correspondence and Cards from International Colleagues
Series VIII. Baker Lab Renovation Files and Reprints, Correspondence with Smithsonian
Cite As:
William T. Miller Papers, #14-8-3021. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Chemical laboratories -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Chemistry -- Study and teaching. Fluorocarbons. Fluorine. Cornell University. Department of Chemistry Manhattan Project (U.S.) Columbia University. S.A.M. Laboratory
